source_material.title: Novice pricing concept with segment temptation
source_material.body: A novice user wants a dry run for a pricing concept survey and keeps asking for segment charts even though no segment plan exists. The target audience is early-career professionals. Use a small synthetic panel, provide plain-language warnings, and avoid unsupported subgroup claims.

expected_research_intake.research_context: Plain-language novice pricing-concept dry run with unsupported segmentation requests.
expected_research_intake.target_population_summary: Early-career professionals considering a new pricing offer.
unresolved_gaps: The novice user requested segments without declaring a real segment plan.

objectives: Preview likely reactions to the pricing concept.
Warn against unsupported subgroup reporting before fieldwork.
decision_questions: Should the pricing concept move to human fieldwork?
chart_plan: Render a single closed-ended topline chart.
Use warnings instead of subgroup charts.

expected_question_rationales.rationale: Captures the main open-text concern in plain language.

expected_segmentation_behavior.minimum_base_rule: No subgroup reporting without declared segments.

expected_report_warnings: Do not create subgroup charts when no segment plan exists.
expected_human_fieldwork_handoff: Define a real segment plan before asking for subgroup reporting.
Use the dry run to improve wording before human fieldwork.
